steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states that Otomi “is not as widely spoken as Tzeltal” but “also has a robust number of speakers.” The blank must name Otomi’s speaker count: a number less than Tzeltal’s 556,700 but large enough to be described as “robust.” The table is the decisive clue.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘134,100 people in Mexico speak it.’: that is Me’phaa’s count, not Otomi’s. Using the wrong language’s data does not complete the statement correctly. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘556,700 people in Mexico speak it.’: that is Tzeltal’s count. Using it for Otomi would contradict the passage’s claim that Otomi is not as widely spoken as Tzeltal. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘307,900 people in Mexico speak it.’: the table shows Otomi at 307,900 speakers — less than Tzeltal’s 556,700, consistent with “not as widely spoken,” and large enough to qualify as “robust.” ✓
  • Choice D — ‘73,856 people in Mexico speak it.’: that is Rarámuri’s count, not Otomi’s, and 73,856 is too small to be described as a “robust” number relative to Tzeltal. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
